meta.setting: Mud roads, wagon ruts, swollen clay, broken axles, mule teams, and delayed freight between French Gulch and the outlying claims
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with road delay, wagon trouble, weather pressure, ambush timing, and hard travel choices

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Rugged road travel, mining, logging, ranching, and settlement pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining-town commerce and freight movement context.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Mud Roads region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Wheel Rut, Swollen Clay, and Axle Groan
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: road object, weather or track detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing mud roads as travel obstacle, schedule breaker, evidence field, ambush timer, and bargaining pressure
closing blockquote that gives a generated mud-road sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate mud roads as active travel pressure: wheel ruts, swollen clay, stuck freight, steaming horses, snapped traces, wet boots, soaked canvas, and road crews who charge because they can.
Give the table hooks around a wagon sunk to the hubs, payroll delayed by rain, a stage forced to detour, boot tracks filling with water, or a rider waiting where everyone must slow down.
Make mud readable; it should show direction, weight, number of animals, time since rain, and whether someone dragged cargo or a body off the road.
Let delay create conflict: food spoils, tempers rise, creditors wait, company guards grow nervous, and a shortcut starts sounding worth the risk.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Mud should make every mile feel owned by the road.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A mud-road scene should ask what is stuck, who cannot wait, what tracks will wash out, and who benefits from the delay.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western road, freight, weather, and tracking language only.
The generated page must include image path images/mud-roads.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the road a mood piece without practical consequences
do not include automobiles, asphalt, concrete culverts, modern road signs, rubber tires, or road-construction machinery
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about mud roads as travel pressure, evidence, delay, and scene engine.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
